title: [Bug]: Oracle MySQL table identifier validation allows too many parts
summary: Oracle MySQL table identifier validation allowed more than table or database.table.
observed: An identifier with multiple dots could pass the allow-list check.
expected: Table identifiers must be limited to table or database.table.
reproduction: Run the focused Oracle MySQL hardening regression test for table identifier depth.
impact: Over-qualified identifiers are outside the documented contract and make SQL generation harder to review.
delivery_semantics: The fix rejects invalid table configuration before delivery work starts.
security: A small identifier grammar keeps dynamic SQL assembly reviewable.
acceptance: Oracle MySQL table identifiers reject values with more than two parts.
Single table and database.table identifiers remain accepted.
tests: Run the focused Oracle MySQL hardening regression test and the Oracle MySQL SQL tests.
closeout: Close only after the release containing the identifier validation fix is published.
